user_query:
Build me a support-ready developer documentation search pack from the markdown files under input/. Do the following:

1) Index all markdown files under input/docs/ and input/tutorials/ into two separate collections using a local file-indexing search that supports both keyword and semantic queries, plus retrieving document excerpts by path:line:length. Use relative workspace paths only.

2) Run these queries: "authentication flow", "rate limiting", "index update", "semantic search", and "API token scopes". For each query, capture top results from keyword search and semantic search, then produce a reranked final top 5. Save all of this to output/query_results.json with this schema:
{
  "queries": [
    {
      "query": "...",
      "bm25_top": ["input/...", "input/..."],
      "vector_top": ["input/...", "input/..."],
      "hybrid_top": ["input/...", "input/...", "input/...", "input/...", "input/..."]
    }
  ]
}

3) For the top hit of each query, extract a 40-line excerpt starting near the matched section and include a citation like "[source: path:line]". Save these excerpts to output/excerpts.md. Keep paths relative (e.g., input/docs/authentication.md:120) and avoid absolute paths.

4) Create output/playbook.md summarizing answers for each query in clear, support-facing language. For each query, include the following subsections: "Short answer", "Key steps", and "Gotchas". Each section must include at least one inline citation like "[source: path:line]" pointing to a relevant excerpt.

5) Save an index status report to output/index_status.json listing the collection names, the glob masks used (e.g., "**/*.md"), and the number of documents indexed in each. Include an "updated_at" timestamp.

All outputs must be written under output/. Read only from input/ and do not include any absolute paths in outputs.
